segments.text: Hello, I'm Peter Bang, working as a chaplain at Eminent Hospice.
The first time I started hospice work was in 2012.
I started working in the US hospice for the first time.
At that time, I learned about the US hospice system.
And I met various patients.
So I realized that hospice work is very precious and important.
It's been about 10 years.
I'm not working alone in hospice.
I work as a team.
I mainly work as a doctor, nurse, social worker, chaplain, and other nurses.
I work as a team.
I think chaplain can be divided into two major roles.
One is the SCS, future counselor, and BC.
There are two areas of treatment.
As you know, there are many patients who work in hospice.
There is a lot of difficulty in their life.
If we control the pain when there is a physical pain, the quality of life is improved a lot.
Likewise, as there is a physical pain, many people live with a spiritual pain in their heart.
I think there are a lot of people who can't think of it as important.
If you get control through counseling, the quality of life is improved a lot.
I think there are more people who have suffered from hospice's death.
Especially, hospice chaplain service is very precious.
It's about taking care of those who are isolated and counseling.
Through that, you can get rid of the pain in your heart.

I remember one of the patients who was in the hospice.
One of the patients was trying to commit suicide while receiving care.
It was a person who tried to stop his own life because he had a lot of loneliness and sadness.
It was a person who tried to stop his own life because he had a lot of sadness and sadness.
If he had received the hospice service a little more, he would have thought that his life would have changed.
Another person was a person who had already been discharged from the hospital in January this year.
He told her that he loved her.
He was a person who couldn't say that he loved her so easily.
But after he left, I talked to my son and my father in the middle of the conversation.
He said that his father loved him.
When I heard that, I felt a lot of pressure on this matter.
Another person is a person who has received care now.
He is a very active person in the past.
He doesn't have anything.
He thinks everything is perfect.
But a few weeks ago, he told me that he had something he wanted to say to me.
He suddenly changed his face and started crying.
He told me that he couldn't say anything to me.
He also told me that he had a lot of sadness in his heart.
I was really surprised that he had such a weak side.
I was really surprised that he had such a weak side.
But I couldn't say anything to anyone in my heart.
After that, I prayed for him.
After that, I saw his face getting brighter.
I realized that the need for hospice, counseling, and chaplain was a valuable experience.
